title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Execution Trace: Parameterized Query Row Close
summary: Handlers open a new database handle per request, concatenate resource IDs, leak rows, perform multi-step writes outside transactions, and return before commit ambiguity is resolved. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo parameterized query row close increment in context-bound repository and transaction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
